headline: Kiribati FIRST BASELINE CONFIRM at 39.1 / Developing — Pacific atoll state with extreme climate vulnerability as the primary structural signal. 33 islands sitting less than two metres above sea level; NASA sea-level rise projections 15-30 cm by 2050, up to 100 cm by 2100; agriculture, water supply, and health already affected; regular flooding in South Tarawa during king tides and storm surges. China-Kiribati diplomatic relations established 2019 (switch from Taiwan) — geopolitical realignment in the Pacific. Climate-refuge land purchased in Fiji as potential displacement-population refuge — concrete forward-planning signal. Structural challenges: limited institutional capacity, freshwater supply pressure, fisheries pressure. No major in-window (May 9-10) events. Published 39.1 (Developing) is plausible — small atoll state with climate-existential context.
boundaryCase_note: Composite 39.1 is within 1.0 of Functional floor (40.0); boundary case flagged. No directional signal in window justifies movement.
key_evidence: 33 islands sitting less than two metres above sea level — extreme climate-existential vulnerability.
NASA projections: sea level rise 15-30 cm by 2050; 50-100 cm by end of century; worst case 2m could render some land uninhabitable.
Agriculture, water supply, and health already affected by climate change; freshwater supplies under pressure.
Regular flooding in South Tarawa during king tides and storm surges.
China-Kiribati diplomatic relations established September 2019 (switch from Taiwan) — geopolitical realignment in Pacific.
Climate-refuge land purchased in Fiji as potential displacement-population refuge — concrete forward-planning signal.
Structural context: small state, limited institutional capacity, fisheries-dependent economy.

evidence.source: Hydro International — Navigating climate change: Kiribati's efforts to address sea-level rise
evidence.finding: Climate adaptation efforts documented; flooding in South Tarawa during king tides; freshwater pressure.

evidence.source: Earth.org — Sea Level Rise Projection Map: Kiribati
evidence.finding: Structural climate-existential context: 33 islands <2m above sea level; uninhabitability projections.

evidence.source: Australia DFAT — Kiribati climate and disaster resilience
evidence.finding: Australia-Kiribati climate-resilience partnership operational; external resource mobilization.

evidence.source: Carnegie Endowment — Reconsidering Sovereignty Amid the Climate Crisis
evidence.finding: Kiribati climate-refuge land in Fiji documented; sovereignty-amid-displacement framework.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained at published. A1 detection: climate-monitoring infrastructure operational; A4 amplification: active climate-advocacy in international forums. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained at published. E1 affective resonance: state-level acknowledgment of climate-displacement risk; E2 protected scope: climate-refuge land purchase in Fiji signals forward-planning for displaced population. Anchor 3 sustained — distinctive above other dimensions due to climate-displacement preparedness.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 sustained at published. AC1 responsiveness: adaptation efforts documented; AC4 resource mobilization: small-state resource constraints; reliance on Australia/DFAT partnership. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained at published. EQ1 universality: standard small-state coverage; EQ2 protected categories: SOGI and other dimensions limited per regional context. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 sustained at published. B5 multilateral engagement: Pacific Islands Forum, Australia/DFAT partnership, climate advocacy; B3 scope clarity: developing capacity caps anchor 3. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 sustained at published. AB4 accountability-to-external-norms: climate-forum engagement; AB1 harm-acknowledgment: state-level disclosure of climate-displacement risk. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 sustained at published. S2 long-term-impact: climate-refuge land purchase creates 20+ year displacement-preparation structure; S4 structural critique: climate-vulnerability frames structural reform. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 sustained at published. I1 consistency: climate-vulnerability advocacy + concrete adaptation actions = sustained values-action engagement; I2 non-performance: small-state low-reputational-cost institutional actions. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 39.1 = published. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Developing band sustained. BOUNDARY CASE: 0.9 below Functional floor (40.0) — flagged for human-reviewer awareness.

negativeSignals: Climate-existential vulnerability (extreme); freshwater supply pressure; flooding already affecting South Tarawa; fisheries pressure; small-state institutional-capacity limits; geopolitical complexity (China switch 2019); SOGI rights remain limited per regional context.
positiveSignals: Climate-refuge land purchased in Fiji (forward-planning structural signal); Australia/DFAT climate-resilience partnership operational; active climate-advocacy in international forums; Pacific Islands Forum engagement; state-level disclosure of climate-displacement risk; climate adaptation efforts documented.
sector_context: Pacific Island States / Atoll States cluster. Kiribati shares the climate-existential pattern with Tuvalu and Marshall Islands. Cross-reference: Tuvalu (similar climate-vulnerability + governance pattern), Marshall Islands (similar Compact-style structural context). All three published at 39.1 — consistent baseline reflecting the structural climate-vulnerability context.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with climate-vulnerability as primary structural anchor
Boundary case (39.1, within 1.0 of Functional floor) flagged for human-reviewer awareness
open_actions_carried: Watch: Kiribati climate adaptation milestone progress
Watch: any further climate-displacement preparation steps
